evidence: Desktop capture showed the headline, vertical tool rail, collage, and campaign content in the settled first viewport.
Controls were inventoried but live interaction replay was not completed.
Black and dark-red display text remained legible against the white editorial field in desktop evidence.
The responsive viewport capture was not reliable enough to verify mobile clipping or overlap.
Navigation transitions were measured, but reduced-motion behavior was not replayed.
The extracted system excludes logos, characters, QR codes, proprietary fonts, copy, and protected media.
White field, near-black type, restrained red emphasis, vertical navigation, and collage composition form one ink-light profile.
The giant Chinese display hierarchy and layered character collage intentionally dominate the first viewport.
observations.rationale: The composition is grounded in the campaign's editorial world and should not be flattened into a conventional hero.

evidence: The vertical rail and asymmetric editorial collage avoid the default SaaS product-page skeleton.
The collage establishes narrative hierarchy without relying on generic glow, card lift, or floating-tag decoration.
The headline, character treatment, and campaign tools are specific to the game's launch presentation.
The vertical tool rail, hard-edged controls, and display composition are visibly art-directed.
The design intentionally uses a pale editorial field, oversized display type, and restrained red accent.
observations.rationale: These choices form a cohesive ink-light profile rather than a generic luxury template.
